summary: PR 2522 split the previous Crazy Hand ftCh_Init translation unit into many separate ftCrazyHand source/header files, marked the new objects Matching in configure.py, updated GALE01 split ranges for the carved objects, and added a few includes/references in neighboring fighter files. The bot report shows linked-code and matched-data gains with 293 new matches, while also showing many broken matches under the old ftCh_Init unit because symbols moved into the new split units.

key_files.role: Reduced original Crazy Hand monolith from the full 0x80155E18-0x8015BD20 range to the initial 0x80155E18-0x80155FCC object.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: splits.txt changes ftCh_Init text/data/sdata2 ranges

key_files.role: New per-action Crazy Hand source units for Wait, Entry, Damage, Sweep, Walk, Drill, RockCrush, Poke, FingerBeam, BackAirplane, grab/capture/tag, and related action logic.
New per-action headers exposing function prototypes with address comments and GALE01 address-based include guards.
Split metadata changed from one ftCh_Init object to many ftCrazyHand objects with individual .text and data-section ranges.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: config/GALE01/splits.txt adds separate ftCrazyHand object ranges

key_files.role: Build configuration changed Crazy Hand from one NonMatching object to many Matching objects.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: configure.py replaces Object(NonMatching, ftCh_Init.c) with many Object(Matching, ftCrazyHand files)

key_files.role: Neighboring fighter file added include for ftCrazyHand/ftCh_TagCancel.h.
Small fighter motion-state update associated with linked Crazy Hand changes; exact lines not loaded.

what_changed.text: 80 files changed: config/GALE01/splits.txt; configure.py; ftCo_Fall.c; 35 ftCrazyHand .c files; 35 ftCrazyHand .h files; small one-line changes in ftMh_Slam.c, ftMh_TagApplaud.c, ftMh_TagCrush.c, fighter.c, ft_0892.c, ft_0C8C.c; and a 5-line change in ftmotionstates.c.

The previous ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init.c span was carved into separate objects such as ftCh_Wait1_0, ftCh_Entry, ftCh_Damage_0, ftCh_Sweep, ftCh_SweepWait, ftCh_Walk, ftCh_Drill, ftCh_RockCrush_0/1, ftCh_Poke, ftCh_FingerBeam_0, ftCh_FingerBeam, BackAirplane1/2/3, BackCrush, BackDisappear, Wait1_1, Grab, Cancel, Squeezing, Squeeze, Throw, Slam, TagRockPaper, FingerGun2, TagGrab, GrabUnk1_B174, Capture*, ThrownCrazyHand, and TagCancel.
configure.py changed Crazy Hand from one NonMatching ftCh_Init object to many Matching ftCrazyHand objects.

Bot report: matched code +1172 bytes, linked code +24328 bytes, matched data +2416 bytes, linked data +2432 bytes, with 293 new matches. The same report lists 254 broken matches in the old ftCh_Init unit and one unmatched regression for ftCh_Init_80156AD8, consistent with symbols moving out of the former monolithic unit.

smart_moves.text: Carving the large Crazy Hand monolith into action-sized translation units made many functions independently linkable/matching and reduced review scope per action file.
The PR kept split metadata and build configuration in sync: every new split in splits.txt has a corresponding Matching object entry in configure.py in the loaded excerpt.
Function prototypes were moved into per-action headers, reducing the old ftCh_Init.h surface after the monolith split.

decomp_lessons.text: When a formerly monolithic object is split into many translation units, report dashboards may show many 'broken matches' in the old unit even as the new units gain matches; interpret those as relocation/split effects unless there is separate evidence of real regressions.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report shows 293 new matches and 254 broken matches under ftCh_Init
diff_excerpt: ftCh_Init split into many objects

decomp_lessons.text: For large boss/fighter action files, action-state boundaries can be a practical split axis: each action file pairs init and Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll handlers and gets its own split/config entry.
Header ownership should follow the new translation-unit split: callsites add the specific action header they need rather than relying on the former umbrella ftCh_Init.h.
evidence_refs: metadata_json.changed_files: ftCh_Init.h shrinks and many per-action headers are added

naming_conventions.text: Crazy Hand action files use the ftCh_<Action>.c and ftCh_<Action>.h pattern, for example ftCh_BackAirplane1.c/.h and ftCh_TagCancel.c/.h.

Handler names follow the action-handler suffix pattern _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll, with init functions often retaining address-suffixed names like ftCh_Init_801592D4 when fuller semantics are not proven.
New headers in the excerpt use address-based include guards such as GALE01_1592D4 and retain address comments on prototypes.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Use splits.txt to assign exact .text/.data/.sdata/.sdata2 ownership for each carved object before marking the object Matching in configure.py.

Preserve unknown semantic names as address-style or fn_ names during broad linking work; the bot report includes matched symbols such as fn_801588B8 and ftCh_Init_80156AD8, indicating not every linked function was semantically renamed.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report new matches list

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: A local stack padding tactic appears in ftCh_BackAirplane3_Anim as `u8 _[8];`; the PR evidence does not explain why it was required, so it should be treated as a narrow matching artifact rather than a general style.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported GALE01 progress: matched code 67.77% (+0.03%, +1172 bytes), linked code 39.86% (+0.63%, +24328 bytes), matched data 39.65% (+0.20%, +2416 bytes), linked data 33.46% (+0.20%, +2432 bytes), 293 new matches, 254 broken matches, and one unmatched regression item.
Review by jurrejelle was APPROVED with no review body in the loaded evidence.

follow_up_queries: PR 2522 ftCrazyHand split broken matches old ftCh_Init new matches

notes: Strong evidence for PR identity, merge state, changed files, split/config changes, and bot-reported match/link deltas.
Diff excerpt is truncated, so only representative new source bodies were inspected directly; many file-specific code details are known only from changed-file metadata and split/config excerpts.
No PR body and no human review comments were provided; reviewer intent beyond approval is unsupported.
Bot report provides aggregate matching evidence but not per-edit objdiff reasoning or why individual tactics such as stack padding were necessary.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Interpret broken matches carefully after TU splitting
accepted_candidate_records.text: If a PR splits a monolithic object into multiple translation units, dashboard 'broken matches' in the old unit may be expected relocation noise when corresponding new split units gain matches. Check split/config changes before treating the old-unit losses as regressions.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftCh_Init split into many ftCrazyHand objects
text_corpus: bot report 293 new matches and 254 broken matches

accepted_candidate_records.title: Per-action boss/fighter splits can reduce monolith review scope
accepted_candidate_records.text: For large fighter/boss action files, splitting by action-state files with matching init and Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll handlers can make matching progress and header ownership easier to review.

source_update_candidates.title: Crazy Hand split units added under src/melee/ft/chara/ftCrazyHand
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2522 split the former ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init.c range into many per-action Crazy Hand source/header files and corresponding GALE01 split entries, including Wait1_0, Entry, Damage_0, Sweep, SweepWait, Walk, Drill, RockCrush_0/1, Poke, FingerBeam_0, FingerBeam, BackAirplane1/2/3, BackCrush, BackDisappear, Wait1_1, Grab, Cancel, Squeezing_0/1, Squeeze, Throw, Slam, TagRockPaper, FingerGun2, TagGrab, GrabUnk1_B174, Capture*, ThrownCrazyHand, and TagCancel.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path/split ownership fact, but should be curated against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Report interpretation for large TU splits
source_update_candidates.text: When a PR carves a monolithic object into new split units, compare new-unit matches against old-unit broken matches before calling the old-unit losses regressions.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report new matches and broken old ftCh_Init matches
diff_excerpt: splits/config changed from one object to many
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a reusable intake lesson supported by this PR, but broader standard wording should be curator-reviewed.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not generalize stack padding from isolated matching artifacts
source_update_candidates.text: A local `u8 _[8];` padding variable appears in ftCh_BackAirplane3_Anim, but the PR does not explain why it was required. Treat such padding as evidence-requiring narrow matching work, not a naming or style convention.
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate reinforces existing matching-tactics caution; evidence is narrow and should not become a new broad rule without more cases.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all 254 broken matches were harmless; the loaded evidence only supports that many losses are associated with the old ftCh_Init unit after a split, while the bot also reports one unmatched regression item.
Do not infer reviewer rationale from the empty approval.
Do not promote all address-style function names as final semantics; the evidence only shows they were accepted in this PR.
